title: Multilingual consent/explainer templates (plain-language)
context: Rare-cancer communities are international; consent and explainer templates in English only exclude non-English-speaking patients and families. Plain-language translations of the consent templates and any patient/family-facing explainers are HIGH risk-tier: each language version requires per-language oncologist + patient-advocate review and sign-off before publication, and every translation must carry 'education only — not medical advice' notices. This is unscheduled backlog; it must not start without M2 consent templates (consent-001) being merged and HIGH-tier reviewers for each target language being secured. No specific target languages are enumerated yet — they will be determined by the adopting community's needs.
objective: Translate the consent templates and patient/family-facing explainers from consent-001 into plain-language versions in the target language(s) identified by the adopting community. Each translation must undergo: (1) clinical accuracy review by a clinical oncologist fluent in the target language, (2) patient-advocate plain-language review, and (3) 'education only — not medical advice' notice in the target language. Provenance must record the translator, reviewers, and review dates.
acceptanceCriteria: Translations produced for the target language(s) specified by the adopting community; no languages are fabricated beyond those requested.
Each translated consent template and explainer carries a 'education only — not medical advice' notice in the target language.
Per-language clinical oncologist sign-off recorded, version-scoped.
Per-language patient-advocate plain-language review recorded, version-scoped.
Provenance records translator identity/method, reviewer names (with consent), review dates, and the source version translated.
Translations are clearly labelled as translations of the source version; changes from the source are documented.
output: Translated consent and explainer templates in `i18n/<language-code>/` directories, each with per-language 'not medical advice' notices and reviewer sign-off records, committed via PR.
